title: fraxinus japonica var. pubinervis
intro: Fraxinus japonica var. pubinervis, also known as the Japanese ash or pubinervis ash, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its compound leaves and greenish-yellow flowers. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of Fraxinus japonica var. pubinervis have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular well-being. Fraxinus japonica var. pubinervis is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
